prompt: Using ProteinMPNN, design a panel of sequence variants for the receptor-binding domain IV of Bacillus anthracis protective antigen (PA domain IV, C-terminal beta-barrel of P13423) to engineer enhanced neutralising epitopes for next-generation anthrax subunit vaccine development, while maintaining anthrax toxin receptor (ATR/CMG2) binding competence for functional verification. Apply entropy maximisation at predicted surface-exposed loops of domain IV while preserving the core beta-barrel fold. Outline a computational approach covering: (1) PA domain IV backbone preparation from PDB 1ACC chain A (C-terminal receptor-binding domain), (2) ProteinMPNN sampling (temperature 0.3) with ATR-contact residue mask to preserve fold-competent sequences, (3) ESMFold structure validation and BepiPred-3.0 B-cell epitope scoring for immunogenicity prediction, (4) selection of top-15 variants for yeast-surface display binding verification with ATR-Fc fusion protein. Include note on CDC/USDA Tier 1 select agent classification for Bacillus anthracis; domain IV alone is non-toxic and can be expressed in E. coli at BSL-1.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05) and RCSB PDB (1ACC); target: Protective antigen PA receptor-binding domain IV; organism: Bacillus anthracis; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_class: anthrax toxin receptor-binding beta-barrel domain (select agent Tier 1)
